questionBank.question: If x=t^2 and y=t^3, then dy/dx=

choices.B: 2t
choices.C: (dy/dt)/(dx/dt)=3t/2

questionBank.notesSnippet: For parametric curves, divide derivatives with respect to t.
questionBank.timeTip: Compute dx/dt and dy/dt separately.

questionBank.question: For parametric x(t),y(t), d^2y/dx^2 equals:

choices.A: d/dt(dy/dx)
choices.B: (d/dt(dy/dx))/(dx/dt)
choices.C: (dy/dt)/(d^2x/dt^2)
choices.D: d^2y/dt^2

questionBank.explanation: Second derivative in parametric form divides by dx/dt again.
questionBank.notesSnippet: Apply chain rule with respect to x via t.
questionBank.timeTip: Write formula before plugging numbers.

questionBank.question: Area enclosed by r=f(theta) from a to b is:

choices.A: int_a^b r dtheta
choices.B: (1/2)int_a^b r^2 dtheta
choices.C: pi r^2
choices.D: int_a^b r^2 dr

questionBank.explanation: Polar area formula is one-half integral of r^2.
questionBank.notesSnippet: Use theta bounds and square r.
questionBank.timeTip: Do not forget the 1/2.

questionBank.question: If x=r cos theta and y=r sin theta, then dy/dx=

choices.A: (dy/dtheta)/(dx/dtheta)
choices.B: dy/dr
choices.C: dx/dy
choices.D: tan(theta)

questionBank.explanation: Differentiate x and y with respect to theta then divide.
questionBank.notesSnippet: Polar slope parallels parametric slope idea.
questionBank.timeTip: Treat theta as parameter.

questionBank.question: If r=2 on 0<=theta<=pi, area is:

choices.A: 2pi
choices.B: pi
choices.C: 4pi
choices.D: 8pi

questionBank.explanation: A=(1/2)int_0^pi 4 dtheta=(1/2)(4pi)=2pi.
questionBank.notesSnippet: Constant radius still uses polar area formula.
questionBank.timeTip: Integrate over theta interval.

frqBank.title: Unit 9 FRQ - Parametric and Polar
frqBank.context: A curve is given parametrically by x=t^2+1, y=t^3-3t.

parts.label: (a)
parts.question: Find dy/dx at t=2.
steps: dx/dt=2t, dy/dt=3t^2-3.
At t=2: dx/dt=4, dy/dt=9.
dy/dx=9/4.
parts.scoring: 1 pt derivatives, 1 pt substitution, 1 pt ratio.

parts.label: (b)
parts.question: Find equation of tangent line at t=2.
steps: Point at t=2: x=5, y=2.
Slope from part (a) is 9/4.
Use point-slope form.
parts.scoring: 1 pt point, 1 pt slope, 1 pt equation.

9.1.title: 9.1 Parametric Basics
9.1.content:
Core formulas

dy/dx=(dy/dt)/(dx/dt), and tangent lines use parametric point + slope.

9.3.title: 9.3-9.4 Polar Area and Slope
9.3.content:
Polar AP formulas

Area = 1/2 int r^2 dtheta, slope uses x(theta), y(theta) derivatives.

referenceHtml:
Unit 9 — Materials on this site include 4 notes PDFs, 2 homework PDFs, and 0 review PDFs. Open Materials (PDFs) in the sidebar for the full list.

The formula blocks below are the shared AP Calc AB reference; your unit packets explain how they show up in your course.
referenceBlurb: Shared AB formulas plus a short pointer to this unit’s PDF materials.

flashcards.back: If lim a_n ≠ 0, then Σ a_n diverges.
